reasoning: Liu Zige has held the women's 200m butterfly long course world record (2:01.81) since October 2009. The greatest current threat to this record is Summer McIntosh, who swam a 2:01.99 at the World Aquatics Championships on July 31, 2025. Because the World Championships have just concluded, the long course swimming calendar enters a dormant period during the fall and winter, where elite swimmers focus on short course meets (e.g., the World Cup series) or heavy training blocks. While some long course meets like the US Open (December) and Pro Swim Series (early 2026) take place, athletes rarely taper fully for these events. Given that McIntosh narrowly missed the record while peaked for the World Championships, it is highly unlikely she breaks it during this off-season period. The probabilities reflect a near-certainty in the short term, with a slight decrease for the winter and early spring of 2026 to account for potential un-tapered breakthrough swims.
